title: Quisqualis pellegriniana
taxonomy: Quisqualis pellegriniana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Combretaceae. It is native to Madagascar, a country located off the coast of East Africa. The plant is also known as the Rangoon creeper or snake flower due to its unique, snake-like tendrils. Within the Combretaceae family, Quisqualis pellegriniana is part of the genus Quisqualis. This classification helps scientists understand the plant's evolutionary history and relationships with other species.

The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and protect against infections.
